title: ExposureReport
description:
A per-principal exposure report for the JSON / text output.

Carries the (baseline-subtracted) findings plus the managed flag, the DAC-only
caveat, and the mounts the walk skipped. Its serialized shape is the locked
`exposure-report.schema.json` contract.

findings.description:
The findings: excess access for a managed principal, raw reachability for a
non-managed uid.

managed.description: Whether the principal is Census-managed (baseline subtraction applied).

principal.description: The principal the report is for.

skipped_mounts.description:
Mounts the walk did not descend (network/pseudo), so the reader knows coverage
was trimmed.

AclPerms.description: The three POSIX permission bits of one ACL entry.

Finding.description:
One exposure finding: a reachable access classified, scored, and given a
remediation path. Serializable for the JSON output (its shape is part of the locked
`exposure-report.schema.json` contract).

access.description: The principal's effective `read`/`write`/`execute` on the inode.

principal.description: The principal the exposure is under, or `None` in the global posture map.

remediation_class.description: Whether the fix is ambient (manual) or in-model (narrow a declaration).

via.description:
The precedence class that granted the access. Serializes (and is schematized) as
its single `via` token string (e.g. `acl_user:role-x`), matching the custom
`Serialize`.

ObjectClass.description:
The security class of an indexed inode, used to derive a finding's risk and
severity.

Assigned by [`classify_object`](crate::exposure::classify_object) from a path-glob
table plus the setuid/setgid mode bits. The token (`as_str`) is the stable form
used in JSON output and filters.

oneOf.description: A cron job or spool entry (scheduled execution as another user).
A systemd unit file (defines a privileged service).
A binary on a system `PATH` directory (run by other users/services).
A setuid/setgid binary (runs with elevated identity).
Anything not matched by a more specific class.

RemediationClass.description: How a finding should be remediated.

oneOf.description:
The access comes from a foreign object Census does not own (its mode / owner /
ACL). Census cannot fix it by changing a declaration; the hint is a manual
`chmod` / `setfacl`.

The access comes from an object Census owns (a managed group, or a path under a
Census file-access grant). The hint is to narrow the declaration.

Risk.description: The kind of harm an exposure enables.

oneOf.description:
Write access that lets the principal gain higher privilege (run code as
another user / root: cron, sudoers, units, path binaries, setuid, secrets).
Read access to a secret-bearing object (credential disclosure).

Write access that lets the principal corrupt configuration or data without a
direct privilege gain.

Severity.description: How urgently a finding warrants attention.

oneOf.description: Direct, high-impact exposure (escalation on a privileged class, secret leak).
Meaningful but bounded (tamper of a security-relevant config).

SkippedMount.description:
A mount point the walk did not descend, recorded so the operator knows coverage
was trimmed (a silent blind spot is unacceptable in a security audit).

fstype.description: The filesystem type that caused the skip (a network or pseudo type).

path.description: Absolute mount-point path that was not descended.
